owasp_top10: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
mitre_attack: T1059.007 - Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript
cwe: CWE-1321 - Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes (Prototype Pollution)
CWE-915 - Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes

threat.title: Prototype Pollution Leading to Remote Code Execution in Node.js Applications
threat.description: Prototype pollution allows attackers to inject properties into JavaScript Object.prototype, affecting all objects application-wide. By manipulating merge operations in libraries like lodash, attackers can poison the prototype chain and achieve denial of service, authentication bypass, or remote code execution. This vulnerability is particularly dangerous in Node.js server-side applications where polluted prototypes can compromise security controls and enable arbitrary code execution.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies endpoint accepting JSON input processed by vulnerable merge function (lodash.merge, _.defaultsDeep)
2. Sends crafted payload with __proto__ or constructor.prototype keys to pollute Object.prototype
3. Injects malicious properties like isAdmin: true or shell command properties
4. Polluted prototype affects all subsequent object creations and property checks
5. Bypasses authentication/authorization checks or triggers command execution via polluted properties
6. Achieves remote code execution through environment variable pollution or child_process exploitation
prerequisites: Application uses vulnerable lodash version (<4.17.12) or similar merge libraries
User-controlled input is merged into objects without sanitization
Application performs property existence checks on objects without hasOwnProperty

cve_references.description: Prototype pollution in lodash defaultsDeep function allowing modification of Object.prototype properties leading to DoS or RCE
Prototype pollution in lodash merge, mergeWith, and defaultsDeep functions due to incomplete fix of CVE-2018-3721
Prototype pollution in lodash _.unset and _.omit functions allowing deletion of prototype methods causing application crashes

real_world_breaches.incident: Parse Server RCE via Prototype Pollution
real_world_breaches.impact: CVE-2022-24760 with CVSS 10.0 - critical RCE in default MongoDB configuration affecting all parse-server versions below 4.10.7
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Prototype pollution in Parse Query processing allowed arbitrary code execution without authentication

real_world_breaches.impact: CVE-2019-7609 - Remote code execution through prototype pollution in Kibana console (.es() function)
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Prototype pollution allowed injection into child_process.spawn() via polluted environment variables

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Prototype pollution in Node.js third-party modules leading to RCE

security_research.title: Server-side Prototype Pollution: Black-box Detection without DoS
security_research.key_finding: Server-side prototype pollution can be detected and exploited without causing denial of service, enabling stealthy RCE attacks

security_research.title: Prototype Pollution Exploitation in Node.js: Multi-staged Static Analysis Framework
security_research.key_finding: First multi-staged framework using static taint analysis to identify prototype pollution vulnerabilities leading to RCE in Node.js applications

mitigations.title: Use Object.create(null) for Configuration Objects
mitigations.description: Create objects without prototype inheritance using Object.create(null) to prevent prototype pollution attacks. Objects created this way have no prototype chain, making them immune to __proto__ and constructor.prototype injection.
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration of information technology/industrial control systems is created and maintained
PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Test payload: {"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}} - should not pollute Object.prototype
Verify Object.prototype.isAdmin remains undefined after merge
Check that config objects have null prototype using Object.getPrototypeOf(obj) === null
Validate nested object merging doesn't allow prototype pollution

mitigations.title: Freeze Object.prototype and Use Safe Merge Libraries
mitigations.description: Freeze JavaScript prototypes at application startup to prevent modification, and replace vulnerable lodash functions with secure alternatives like safe-merge or custom validators.
nist_csf: PR.IP-12 - A vulnerability management plan is developed and implemented
validation_steps: npm audit to verify lodash version >= 4.17.21
Test frozen prototypes: attempt Object.prototype.polluted = true in strict mode (should throw)
Send malicious payload: {"__proto__": {"polluted": true}} - should be rejected
Verify error handling returns 400 status with appropriate message

mitigations.title: Implement Schema Validation with JSON Schema
mitigations.description: Use JSON Schema validation libraries (AJV, express-validator) to enforce strict input validation and reject any properties not explicitly defined in the schema, preventing prototype pollution through unrecognized keys.
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Define JSON schemas for all API endpoints accepting JSON input
Set additionalProperties: false in all schemas
Test with malicious payload containing __proto__ - should be rejected with 400
Verify valid payloads are accepted and invalid ones return detailed error messages

indicators: Unexpected properties appearing on Object.prototype across the application
TypeError: Cannot read property 'X' of undefined after object merging operations
Sudden authentication bypasses or privilege escalation without credential changes
Application crashes with 'Maximum call stack size exceeded' errors
Unexpected command execution or child_process spawning
Environment variable pollution detected in process.env

log_queries.query:
// Detect prototype pollution attempts in API requests
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where CsMethod in ("POST", "PUT", "PATCH")
| where CsUriStem contains "/api/"
| extend RequestBody = parse_json(tostring(CsBody))
| where RequestBody contains "__proto__" or RequestBody contains "constructor" and RequestBody contains "prototype"
| extend ClientIP = CIp, Endpoint = CsUriStem
| summarize AttemptCount = count(), UniqueIPs = dcount(ClientIP), Endpoints = make_set(Endpoint) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where AttemptCount > 5
| project TimeGenerated, AttemptCount, UniqueIPs, Endpoints
| order by AttemptCount desc

sequence by source.ip, host.name with maxspan=10m
  [network where event.category == "web" and http.request.method in ("POST", "PUT", "PATCH")]
  [network where http.request.body.content like "*__proto__*" or http.request.body.content like "*constructor*prototype*"]
  [process where event.type == "start" and process.parent.name == "node"]

index=nodejs_app sourcetype=express_json
| rex field=request_body "(?<proto_attempt>__proto__|constructor.*prototype)"
| where isnotnull(proto_attempt)
| stats count as attempts dc(src_ip) as unique_ips values(uri) as endpoints by proto_attempt, bin(_time, 1h)
| where attempts > 3
| eval severity=case(attempts > 20, "critical", attempts > 10, "high", attempts > 5, "medium", 1=1, "low")
| table _time, proto_attempt, attempts, unique_ips, endpoints, severity
| sort -attempts

fields @timestamp, @message, httpRequest.method, httpRequest.path, requestBody
| filter httpRequest.method in ["POST", "PUT", "PATCH"]
| filter requestBody like /__proto__/ or requestBody like /constructor.*prototype/
| stats count() as attempts, count_distinct(httpRequest.remoteAddr) as unique_ips by bin(1h) as time_window
| filter attempts > 5
| sort attempts desc
